goal: Land the G1 parallel-execution orchestrator that fans out a structural query to tree-sitter, Serena, ast-grep, and the LSP diagnostics bridge concurrently with `tokio::join!` and a 5 s overall deadline, returning per-source results (Available / Unavailable / TimedOut / Errored) so partial results are usable when a tool is unavailable.
plan_summary: Add `G1Source`, `G1Query`, `G1ToolKind`, `G1ToolStatus`, `G1ToolOutput`, `G1Outcome`, and `pub async fn execute_g1(query, sources, deadline)` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` as a new public surface alongside the existing WO-0037 `SerenaHoverClient` / `enrich_find_definition` pair. Orchestrator collects four `Box<dyn G1Source + Send + Sync + 'static>` boxed-trait sources, wraps each in `tokio::time::timeout(per_source_deadline, ...)` inside a `tokio::join!(...)` block (per master-plan §5.1 and §18 Phase-2 Week-7 line 1780), and synthesises a `G1Outcome { results: [G1ToolOutput; 4], wall_elapsed_ms, master_timed_out }`. The frozen acceptance selector `executor::test_g1_parallel_execution` lands at MODULE ROOT of `executor.rs` per DEC-0007, asserts (a) parallel wall-time < 1.5× the slowest source delay (lower bound: parallelism happened; upper bound: master deadline did not trigger), (b) one-source-Errored partial outcome is reachable, and (c) one-source-TimedOut partial outcome is reachable. Per DEC-0008, the unit acceptance test injects local trait impls of `G1Source` (UCIL's own abstraction boundary) — production wiring of real subprocess clients (Serena MCP plugin, ast-grep MCP plugin, real LSP, real `ucil_treesitter`) is deferred to P2-W7-F02 (fusion) + P2-W7-F05 (find_references) per the master-plan dependency chain. `lib.rs` re-exports for every new `pub` type land in this WO (no deferred re-export debt — carry-forward from WO-0042 / WO-0043 / WO-0044 / WO-0045 / WO-0046 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline).
scope_in: Create `pub trait G1Source: Send + Sync` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` with one method `async fn execute(&self, query: &G1Query) -> G1ToolOutput` and an associated `fn kind(&self) -> G1ToolKind` (so the orchestrator can label results by source without runtime introspection). The trait is UCIL's own boundary type per DEC-0008 spirit — the same pattern as `SerenaHoverClient` (`executor.rs:640`).

Add `pub struct G1Query { pub symbol: String, pub file_path: PathBuf, pub line: u32, pub column: u32 }` — the structural-query input shape. Mirrors the master-plan §5.1 fan-out target (`Query → ALL of the following run in parallel`).

Add `pub enum G1ToolKind { TreeSitter, Serena, AstGrep, Diagnostics }` (4 variants, one per master-plan §5.1 G1 source) with `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize`. SCIP and Joern are EXPLICITLY out of scope here (P2-W7-F08 lands SCIP, post-Phase-2 lands Joern).

Add `pub enum G1ToolStatus { Available, Unavailable, TimedOut, Errored }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize`. Each variant is a discriminant with no inner data — the data lives on `G1ToolOutput`.

Add `pub struct G1ToolOutput { pub kind: G1ToolKind, pub status: G1ToolStatus, pub elapsed_ms: u64, pub payload: serde_json::Value, pub error: Option<String> }`. `payload` is the source's emitted JSON (e.g. tree-sitter AST snippet, Serena hover, ast-grep matches, diagnostics bundle); `error` is `Some(msg)` for `Unavailable`/`TimedOut`/`Errored` and `None` for `Available`.

Add `pub struct G1Outcome { pub results: Vec<G1ToolOutput>, pub wall_elapsed_ms: u64, pub master_timed_out: bool }`. Vec rather than `[G1ToolOutput; 4]` so the same orchestrator can be reused unchanged when SCIP/Joern land in P2-W7-F08 / Phase-3.

Add `pub const G1_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000);` and `pub const G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500);` — 4.5 s per source so the master 5 s deadline always wins on a true global stall (avoids the per-source-timeout fast-path racing the master deadline). Cite §5.1 line 444 in the const rustdoc.

Add `pub async fn execute_g1<S>(query: G1Query, sources: Vec<Box<S>>, deadline: Duration) -> G1Outcome where S: G1Source + ?Sized` — the orchestrator. Implementation: spawn each source via `tokio::time::timeout(min(deadline, G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE), s.execute(&query))` inside a `let (a, b, c, d) = tokio::join!(...)` block (or `futures::future::join_all(...)` if the source count is dynamic — `join_all` is acceptable here because the test asserts wall-time, not the literal `tokio::join!` macro). Master-deadline guard: wrap the whole `join_all` in an outer `tokio::time::timeout(deadline, ...)`; on `Err(Elapsed)`, return `G1Outcome { results: <whatever-completed-so-far OR placeholder TimedOut entries>, wall_elapsed_ms, master_timed_out: true }`. NEVER `panic!`, NEVER `?` out of the orchestrator — partial results are valid output per master-plan §5.1.

Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_g1_parallel_execution()` annotated `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The test body covers THREE assertions in one function (single frozen selector — no separate tests):  
  (a) **Parallel timing**: all 4 sources sleep 200 ms each via `tokio::time::sleep` in their `execute` impls; assert `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms >= 180` (lower bound: at least one source's sleep elapsed) AND `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 600` (upper bound: serial would be >800 ms; 600 ms ceiling proves parallel). Dual-bound discipline carried from WO-0043 lessons line 109. Cite the bound in the assertion message.  
  (b) **Partial-Errored**: one of the 4 sources returns `G1ToolOutput { status: G1ToolStatus::Errored, error: Some("injected".into()), .. }`; assert the outcome contains exactly 1 `Errored` entry AND 3 `Available` entries.  
  (c) **Partial-TimedOut**: one of the 4 sources sleeps 6 s (longer than `G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE`); assert that source's entry is `G1ToolStatus::TimedOut` and the others are `Available`. The whole test must complete in < 7 s wall-time (per-source 4.5 s ceiling means the slow source resolves to TimedOut at 4.5 s; assert `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 5_500`).

Update `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` `pub use executor::{...}` line (currently `executor.rs:102`) to add the 7 new public symbols: `G1Source`, `G1Query`, `G1ToolKind`, `G1ToolStatus`, `G1ToolOutput`, `G1Outcome`, `execute_g1`, plus the two `Duration` consts `G1_MASTER_DEADLINE` and `G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE`. Lib.rs preamble paragraph for WO-0037 (lines 51-62) gets a NEW trailing sentence: 'WO-0047 for `P2-W7-F01` extends [`executor`] with the [`G1Source`] dependency-inversion seam (per `DEC-0008`) and [`execute_g1`], the parallel orchestrator that fans out a structural query to tree-sitter / Serena / ast-grep / diagnostics-bridge per master-plan §5.1 and returns a `G1Outcome` with per-source [`G1ToolStatus`] (Available / Unavailable / TimedOut / Errored). Production wiring of real subprocess clients lands in P2-W7-F02 (fusion) and P2-W7-F05 (find_references); F01 ships the orchestrator + the trait only.' Pre-emptively rewrites the deferral-signpost the WO-0045 lessons (line 218) called out as load-bearing.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F01.sh` per the standing convention (operator-actionable failure paths). Script must (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_parallel_execution -- --nocapture`, (b) tee output through a regex matching either `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests run: ... passed` shape (alternation per WO-0042 lessons line 75), (c) emit `[FAIL] P2-W7-F01: <reason>` on non-zero exit and reference the specific assertion line on test failure.

Compose with existing `enrich_find_definition` (WO-0037) WITHOUT removing it. The G1-fusion-lite hover-only helper stays as the current `find_definition` MCP tool surface; F02 (G1 fusion) will compose `execute_g1`'s outputs into a richer fused payload. F01 adds capability without breaking the existing call site at `server::McpServer::handle_find_definition`. Cite the explicit non-removal in the executor preamble doc-comment so the next reader does not double-take.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: the per-source mutation patches must use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 — `let _ = arg;` for any arg that the mutated body discards, plus a no-op `tokio::task::yield_now().await;` to satisfy `clippy::unused_async`. Document BOTH the literal sed AND the runtime-only variant in `acceptance` mutation entries below.

Optional but recommended: a per-source rustdoc paragraph naming each `G1ToolKind` variant's expected production wiring source (TreeSitter → `ucil_treesitter::Parser`, Serena → `executor::SerenaHoverClient`, AstGrep → ast-grep MCP plugin per WO-0044, Diagnostics → `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics::bridge`) so the F02 / F05 executor does not have to re-derive the wiring map. Backticks every uppercase identifier in rustdoc per the WO-0043 `clippy::doc_markdown` lesson.
scope_out: **No production wiring** of real Serena MCP, ast-grep MCP, real `ucil_treesitter::Parser`, or real `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics::bridge` clients. F01 ships only the orchestrator + trait + 4 local trait impls in the unit test. F02 (G1 fusion) and F05 (find_references) are the natural homes for the production wiring.

**No fusion logic**. F01's `G1Outcome.results` is a per-source raw vector; merging by source location, conflict resolution, source-authority ordering (Serena > tree-sitter > ast-grep) all land in P2-W7-F02. The acceptance test asserts on the raw per-source results, not on a fused single output.
**No SCIP / Joern integration**. P2-W7-F08 lands SCIP P1; Joern is post-Phase-2. `G1ToolKind` enum has 4 variants only; extending the enum is an additive non-breaking change later WOs can make.
**No MCP-tool wiring**. `find_definition` continues to use WO-0037's `enrich_find_definition` (Serena hover only) until F02 fans it out through `execute_g1`. `find_references` is F05's job.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`** — F01 is single-crate (ucil-daemon) by design.

**No `tokio::join!` literal-macro requirement**. The acceptance test asserts on wall-time and per-source statuses, not on the executor's choice between `tokio::join!` and `futures::future::join_all`. Either is acceptable; `join_all` is preferred when the source vec is dynamic-sized.

**No retry / backoff logic on the orchestrator path**. F01 ships single-shot fan-out with timeout. Per-source restart-on-failure (e.g. via `PluginManager::restart_with_backoff` from WO-0043) is the orchestrator's caller's concern, not F01's.

**No new ADR**. DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal trait boundaries OK to local-impl in tests), DEC-0010 (workspace integration crate — not used here since the acceptance selector is unit), DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits) cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (cross-task panics, lifetime issues with boxed-trait + `'static`), STOP and write an ADR before working around.
**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate**. DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046).
acceptance: AC01 — `cargo build -p ucil-daemon` exits 0.

AC02 — `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0. Pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` returns zero hits OR every match is inside backticks (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`).
AC03 — `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_parallel_execution -- --nocapture` exits 0.

AC04 — Test selector `executor::test_g1_parallel_execution` lives at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Verifier checks via `grep -nE '^pub async fn test_g1_parallel_execution' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` returning a non-`mod tests {}`-nested line.

AC05 — `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms` is asserted with BOTH lower bound (`>= 180`, proves at least one source's 200 ms sleep elapsed) AND upper bound (`< 600`, proves serial 4×200 ms = 800 ms did not happen → parallelism confirmed). Dual-bound discipline per WO-0043 lessons line 109.
AC06 — Partial-Errored sub-assertion: outcome has exactly 1 `G1ToolStatus::Errored` and 3 `G1ToolStatus::Available` entries when 1 of 4 trait impls returns `Errored`.

AC07 — Partial-TimedOut sub-assertion: outcome has exactly 1 `G1ToolStatus::TimedOut` and 3 `G1ToolStatus::Available` entries when 1 of 4 trait impls sleeps > `G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE` (4.5 s); whole test wall-time < 5.5 s asserted with `outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 5_500`.

AC08 — Phase-2 Week-6 regression: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine plugin_manager::test_hot_reload plugin_manager::test_circuit_breaker` exits 0.
AC09 — Phase-2 Week-6 integration regression: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manager` exits 0.

AC10 — Phase-2 Week-6 plugin-manifest regression: `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS=1 cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests` exits 0 (pre-flight `unset UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` for the verifier's full run; the AC10 line uses the skip env to keep the executor's local loop fast — verifier removes the skip per WO-0044 lessons line 176).
AC11 — Phase-1 e2e regression: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio --test e2e_mcp_with_kg` exits 0.
AC12 — Phase-2 Week-6 lifecycle integration regression: `cargo test --test test_plugin_lifecycle` exits 0.
AC13 — Phase-1 LSP-bridge regression: `cargo test --test test_lsp_bridge` exits 0.
AC14 — Workspace-wide regression: `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` exits 0.

AC15 — Coverage gate: `bash scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` exits 0. Use the `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER` workaround per WO-0042/0043/0044/0045/0046 lessons line 90 / 132 / 179 / 226 / 270 (now 9th consecutive WO using this protocol; escalation `20260419-0152-monitor-phase1-gate-red-integration-gaps.md` still open).

AC16 — Stub-scan (per phase-2 invariant #1: stdout pristine on stdio MCP path): `rg -nE 'todo!\(\)|unimplemented!\(\)|panic!\(".*not yet|TODO|FIXME' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` returns ZERO new hits relative to `git show main:crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs | rg -cE 'todo!\(\)|unimplemented!\(\)|panic!\(".*not yet|TODO|FIXME'`. (`unwrap()` is permitted ONLY inside `#[cfg(test)]` per root CLAUDE.md.)

AC17 — Allow-list verification (three-dot diff per WO-0046 lessons line 250): `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` lists ONLY paths in this exact set: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`, `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F01.sh`, `ucil-build/work-orders/0047-ready-for-review.md`. Optional 5th entry: `Cargo.lock` (allowed only if `cargo build -p ucil-daemon` updates lockfile metadata; no new dependencies are added so this should be empty in practice).

AC18 — `Cargo.toml` files are NOT modified (no new dependency additions). `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- '*.toml'` returns empty. Verifier confirms no new crate was pulled in for the orchestrator — `tokio` (already a dep), `serde` / `serde_json` (already deps), `futures` (already a workspace dep — confirm via `cargo tree -p ucil-daemon | rg '^futures '` BEFORE writing code; if absent, replace `futures::future::join_all` with `tokio::join!` to avoid the dep add).

AC19 — `lib.rs` re-exports for ALL 9 new public symbols (`G1Source`, `G1Query`, `G1ToolKind`, `G1ToolStatus`, `G1ToolOutput`, `G1Outcome`, `execute_g1`, `G1_MASTER_DEADLINE`, `G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE`) land in this WO — zero deferred re-export debt per the WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline (now 5 consecutive cleared). `grep -nE 'G1Source|G1Query|G1ToolKind|G1ToolStatus|G1ToolOutput|G1Outcome|execute_g1|G1_MASTER_DEADLINE|G1_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` returns ALL 9 symbols.

AC20 — Pre-baked mutation #1 (orchestrator body neutered):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|let outer = tokio::time::timeout(deadline,|let outer: Result<_, tokio::time::error::Elapsed> = Ok(|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (replaces the master-deadline guard).  
  Runtime-only variant (use this if literal sed produces a `#![deny(warnings)]` cascade per WO-0046 lessons line 245): replace the `execute_g1` body with `{ let _ = query; let _ = sources; let _ = deadline; tokio::task::yield_now().await; G1Outcome::default() }` (drops `mut` if any, discards every arg with `let _ = X;`, adds a no-op await for `clippy::unused_async`, returns `Default`).  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_parallel_execution`: panic at the parallel-timing assertion line in `test_g1_parallel_execution` (the dual-bound `wall_elapsed_ms < 600` AND `>= 180` cannot both hold for an empty `Default` outcome — `wall_elapsed_ms` will be 0).  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`. Rerun: green.  
  `G1Outcome` MUST `derive Default` for this mutation patch to compile — bake that derive into the type definition (already standard for outcome types).

AC21 — Pre-baked mutation #2 (per-source timeout wrapper neutered):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|tokio::time::timeout(per_source_deadline,|std::convert::identity::<_>(|' crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` (drops the per-source `tokio::time::timeout` wrapper — note: this likely won't compile because identity preserves the future shape rather than the `Result<_, Elapsed>` shape; the verifier should fall back to the runtime-only variant).  
  Runtime-only variant: replace the per-source future construction `tokio::time::timeout(per_source_deadline, source.execute(query)).await` with `Ok::<_, tokio::time::error::Elapsed>(source.execute(query).await)` so the timeout branch never fires (returns the source's raw output as an `Ok` regardless of how long it took).  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g1_parallel_execution`: the partial-TimedOut sub-assertion at AC07 panics — the slow source completes after 6 s (full sleep) without hitting `G1ToolStatus::TimedOut`, so the assertion `1 TimedOut + 3 Available` fails. Whole test wall-time exceeds 5.5 s ceiling and trips a wall-time budget assertion.  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs`. Rerun: green.
AC22 — `tests/fixtures/**` is NOT modified. `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- 'tests/fixtures/**'` returns empty. Per root CLAUDE.md.

AC23 — `ucil-build/feature-list.json` and `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json` are NOT modified by the executor. `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- 'ucil-build/feature-list.json' 'ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json'` returns empty (verifier flips passes via `scripts/flip-feature.sh` AFTER critic + verifier run).
AC24 — `ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md` is NOT modified. `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- 'ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md'` returns empty.

AC25 — Commit cadence: at least 3 commits on the feature branch (one for types + trait, one for `execute_g1` + per-source timeout logic, one for the acceptance test + lib.rs re-exports). Per root CLAUDE.md commit cadence ('one logical change per commit, max ~50 LOC of diff per commit'). DEC-0005 module-coherence permits up to ~200 LOC for a coherent test+helper commit; 252 LOC was the WO-0046 cap (informational warning, not a blocker).
AC26 — Branch is up-to-date with `origin`: `git rev-parse HEAD` matches `git rev-parse @{u}`. No uncommitted changes (`git status --porcelain` empty).
